Step 1 correctly filters the columns to focus only on the "gold" column, which is the relevant data needed to answer the question about the number of gold medals distributed. Step 1 is correct.

Step 2 incorrectly calculates the total number of gold medals by including the "total" row (row 16) in the sum. The "total" row already represents the sum of gold medals from all nations (45), so adding it again would double-count the medals. The correct sum should only include the gold medals from individual nations (rows 1 to 15), which totals 45. Therefore, Step 2 is incorrect.
